description_long: This skyscraper features a strikingly modern, stacked-block design with six distinct levels, each offset horizontally to create a dynamic, asymmetrical profile. The facade alternates between sleek, dark-tinted glass panels and smooth, light gray cladding, with some levels incorporating wood-toned horizontal bands for visual warmth. Large, floor-to-ceiling windows dominate several floors, suggesting open-plan commercial or office spaces, while other levels are more enclosed with minimal fenestration. The ground floor is set back and features a colonnade or overhang, likely serving as a sheltered entrance or public plaza. The rooftop appears to have a partially open terrace or mechanical area, with railings and a red accent zone. The overall aesthetic is minimalist and geometric, emphasizing clean lines and a bold, cantilevered silhouette.
description: A modern, asymmetrical skyscraper with stacked, offset floors, alternating glass and gray cladding, and wood-toned accents. The design features large windows, a recessed ground floor, and a partially open rooftop terrace.
description_view_0: Front view showing the offset stacked floors, prominent glass panels, and wood-toned bands, with the ground floor set back and a clear view of the rooftop terrace.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the horizontal displacement of each floor, the interplay of solid and transparent surfaces, and the visible rooftop details.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of the stacked blocks, with alternating glass and solid cladding, and a clear view of the lower recessed section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the cantilevered sections, window patterns, and the layered effect of the facade materials.
attribute_facade_design: The facade features alternating bands of dark-tinted glass and smooth gray cladding, with horizontal wood-toned accents adding warmth. The design is minimalist, with clean lines, large window expanses, and a bold, cantilevered block arrangement.
